title: narcissus radiiflorus subsp. exertus
intro: Narcissus radiiflorus subsp. exertus is a subspecies of plant within the Narcissus genus, commonly known as a type of daffodil, characterized by its unique flower shape and white, trumpet-like blooms. Traditionally, Narcissus radiiflorus subsp. exertus has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant are said to include relieving symptoms of anxiety and depression, improving cognitive function, and supporting immune system health. Herbal preparations of Narcissus radiiflorus subsp. exertus can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
